description: A part is 'monae' when a particular whole needs it to remain complete or to function as defined. A 'monaki' (component) is identified by its role in a system, but only some components are integral. 'noetha' (essential) is broader because an external condition can be indispensable without being part of the whole.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm', carry the voice through the nose, and open into true-mid rounded back 'o'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', continue the nasal voice, and open into stressed central 'a'. Move the tongue forward and raise it slightly for a separate true-mid unrounded 'e', keeping the 'a.e' hiatus clear.
sound_symbolism: Voice continues through both nasal consonants while the vowels open from rounded 'o' through 'a' to final 'e'. The three syllables stay audibly joined, like a part that remains inside its whole.
usage_notes: The relevant whole may be stated nearby or already shared. For a person, context supplies the community or activity in which their participation is integral; 'rolia' (worth) remains independent of that role.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A repair plan can identify one integral component and keep the larger device in use instead of discarding the whole assembly.

semantic_domains.cognition: a necessary part-to-whole relation identified in an analysis
semantic_domains.creation: a component a made system needs in order to remain complete or function as defined
